title: Umami Crispy Filipino Salmon Ceviche
description: A zesty Filipino ceviche featuring marinated salmon, fermented umami sauce, and a delightful crispy coating.
instructions: Whisk lime juice, soy sauce, fish sauce, fermented bean paste, and minced garlic in a shallow dish until the paste is fully dispersed.
Cut salmon into 1 cm cubes and immerse them in the citrus‑soy mixture. Cover and refrigerate for 12–15 minutes to cure the fish.
In a small bowl, combine toasted coconut flakes to create a light coating.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat and add a teaspoon of oil. Pat salmon cubes dry, then lightly roll them in the coconut coating; tap off excess.
Sear the coated salmon for 2–3 minutes on each side until crisp and golden, but still raw inside. Transfer to a plate.
Dice cucumber, red onion, and slice the chili; grate the ginger. Toss these vegetables with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Arrange the seared salmon on top of the cucumber‑onion‑chili mixture.
Drizzle the remaining lime‑soy sauce over the top and sprinkle with additional toasted coconut flakes, grated ginger, and a pinch of salt.
Serve immediately, optionally with steamed rice or a light mango salsa.
